concerns.text: Task T018 (functional group annotation) and T019 (backbone flexibility) produce artifacts (covalent_radius, backbone_flexibility_score) that are consumed by T030 and T036. However, T018 and T019 are listed AFTER T014 (Save cleaned dataset) and T013 (Feature extraction). If T014 saves the dataset before T018/T019 run, the saved artifact will lack these critical features required for the GNN input (T030) and statistical analysis (T036). T018/T019 must precede T014 or be integrated into T013 to ensure the saved artifact is complete.

Task T030 (Augment GNN input with Pauling-derived parameters) depends on T018 and T019. While T030 is in Phase 4, the data ingestion tasks (T018/T019) are in Phase 3. The dependency chain is valid, BUT T030 implies the GNN model (T021) needs these features. T021 is listed before T030. If T021 is implemented as a static class expecting specific input dimensions, T030 (which adds features) might require T021 to be refactored or T021 must be implemented *after* T030 defines the feature set. The current order suggests T021 (Model) is built before T030 (Feature Augmentation) defines the full input schema, risking a mismatch in input dimensions.

Task T017 (Temperature normalization) is listed after T014 (Save dataset). If T014 saves the raw/processed data before T017 normalizes the permeability values, the saved artifact will contain un-normalized data. T017 must precede T014 to ensure the saved 'cleaned dataset' contains the normalized target values required for training.

Task T029 (Randomized Topology Control) is listed after T026 (Generate JSON report). The report (T026) likely compares GNN vs Baselines. If T029 is a required baseline for the comparison, T029 must precede T026. The current order implies the report is generated before the topology control baseline is implemented, which would make the report incomplete or require a second run.
